title: Springy Nostalgic Kenyan Beef Bowl
description: A hearty, comforting dish made with tender beef and a rich, savory broth. Served in a rustic earthenware bowl.

ingredients.name: beef brisket
ingredients.notes: unsalted, cured for at least 7 days

ingredients.name: beef stock
ingredients.notes: unsalted, reduced from 2L to concentrate flavors

instructions: Cut the beef brisket into katsuramuki-cut strips, about 1cm thick.
In a large pan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook until they start to caramelize, stirring occasionally.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Add the beef stock, turmeric, cumin, coriander, and salt-free seasoning. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 hour.
Add the kaffir lime leaves and cook for a further 30 minutes.
Add the katsuramuki-cut beef and cook for 10-15 minutes, or until it is tender and springy.
Season with salt-free seasoning to taste.
Serve hot in earthenware bowls, garnished with additional kaffir lime leaves if desired.
